perturbation.original_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information : The Confidant shall immediately on notice at any time from City return to City, or destroy, any and all Confidential In formation in accordance with City’s direction.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information : The Confidant shall on notice from City return to City, or destroy, any and all Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'immediately' and 'in accordance with City’s direction' from Section 4, the timing of the return of Confidential Information and the manner of destruction are now undefined. This creates an ambiguity with Section 14, which states that each of the parties shall do all acts and things and execute from time to time all such further documents and assurances as may be necessary to carry out and give effect to the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Since the section refers that the parties should do all acts in order to give effect to the terms and conditions, now that the term 'immediately', does give effect that the information has to be returned at that moment. By removing that term, now the term and conditions are not completely being carried out because the information might be returned in a larger time period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Further Acts and Assurances : Each of the parties shall, from time to time, do all acts and things and execute from time to time all such further documents and assurances as may be necessary to carry out and give effect to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'immediately' and 'in accordance with City’s direction' from Section 4 introduces ambiguity regarding the timing and manner of returning confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 14, which mandates that parties take all necessary actions to give effect to the agreement's terms and conditions. The term 'immediately' in the original Section 4 clarified the timing, which now is ambiguous. This directly affects the enforcement of the terms and conditions, as the timing and manner are undefined, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No License Granted : Confidant acknowledges and agrees that all rights in and to Confidential Information are and shall remain the sole property of City, and Confidant agrees that it shall not contest or challenge any of City’s rights in or to any Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement obligates, or shall be deemed to obligat e, City to provide, disclose, or deliver any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No License Granted : Confidant acknowledges and agrees that all rights in and to Confidential Information are and shall remain the property of City, and Confidant agrees that it shall not contest or challenge City’s rights in or to any Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement obligates City to disclose Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'sole' before 'property' and removing 'or shall be deemed to obligate' and 'provide, disclose, or deliver' the protection of Confidential Information is diminished. By deleting 'sole', now the City doesn't have the only property on the Confidential information. This introduces a contradiction with Section 8, which states that the Agreement shall be subject to, interpreted, performed and enforced in accordance with the laws of Manitoba and the applicable laws of Canada without regard to Manitoba or Federal Canadian law governing conflicts of law, even if one or more of the parties to this Agreement is resident of or domiciled in any ot her province or country. Section 6's change impacts how Section 8 can be enforced, since Manitoba or Federal Canadian laws usually cover who has the sole property and that laws must be respected to who has the sole property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Governing Law and Interpretation : This Agreement shall be subject to, interpreted, performed and enforced in accordance with the laws of Manit oba and the applicable laws of Canada without regard to Manitoba or Federal Canadian law governing conflicts of law, even if one or more of the parties to this Agreement is resident of or domiciled in any ot her province or country. Section headings in this Agreement are for the convenience of the parties on ly, and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text's Section 6 stated that City has 'sole property' and that nothing obligates City to 'provide, disclose, or deliver' information. The changed text's Section 6 removes 'sole' from 'property' and removes 'provide, disclose, or deliver', thus weakening City's exclusive rights and lessening the protection afforded to confidential information. This weakening impacts Section 8, which mandates adherence to Manitoba and Canadian laws, which often deal with sole property rights. By removing the 'sole' designation, the changed text creates a contradiction with Section 8, as it impacts how the governing laws can be applied to protect City's interest. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with the obligations in Section 8.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Assignment : Confidant shall not assign this Agreement without first having obtained the prior written consent of City. No assignment of this Agreement shall operate so as to relieve Confidant from any obligation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Assignment : Confidant shall not assign this Agreement without the consent of City. No assignment of this Agreement shall operate so as to relieve Confidant from any obligation of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'prior written' from Section 12, the requirement for the consent to be in writing is removed. This introduces a contradiction with Section 11, which states that No amendment or change or modification of this Agreement shall be valid unless it is in writing and signed by both parties. Because an assignment is a change of the Agreement, then the removal of prior written is contradicting the clause where it says that any change has to be in writing
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Amendments : No amendment or change or modification of this Agreement shall be valid unless it is in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 12, by removing 'prior written', eliminates the requirement for the consent to be in writing for an assignment. Section 11 states that any change or modification to the Agreement must be in writing. Since an assignment is a change to the agreement, the modified Section 12 directly contradicts Section 11, because it now allows for a non-written assignment, which is not permitted by section 11. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it creates conflicting requirements regarding the form an assignment must take.
